exemplar.title: How does the internal pressure of a football (25, 40, 55, 70, 85, 100 kPa) affect its maximum rebound height after four consecutive bounces when dropped from a fixed height, and how does the internal pressure affect the coefficient of restitution as a measure of the ballâs elasticity?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does the internal pressure of a football (25, 40, 55, 70, 85, 100 kPa) affect its maximum rebound height after four consecutive bounces when dropped from a fixed height, and how does the internal pressure affect the coefficient of restitution as a measure of the ballâs elasticity?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Mathematical derivation of e=â(h_final/h_initial) is sound and clearly presented
Raw data tables consistently include uncertainties, units, and significant figures
Worked examples for averages and coefficient of restitution calculations are detailed
Processed tables list averages and derived COR values with stated uncertainties
weaknesses: Several uncertainty calculations are incorrect or inconsistent with propagation rules
Error bars are omitted from graphs despite non-zero uncertainties
Graph labels, axis units, and legends are incomplete or misaligned
No quantitative assessment of correlation strength (e.g., fitted trendline or RÂ²)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis is technically appropriate in principle, with clear communication of data recording and processing (band 3â4). However, misapplied uncertainty propagation, missing error bars, formatting inconsistencies, and incomplete graphical presentation introduce significant inaccuracies and omissions, placing it at the lower end of the 3â4 band. A score of 3 reflects these limitations.

strengths: Clear and specific research question grounded in a real-world sports context
Introduction links mechanics concepts (inelastic collisions, energy transformations) to practical training scenarios
Procedure and materials list include key details (pressure values, trial repetition, controlled variables) supporting reproducibility
Hypothesis articulates causal mechanisms between pressure, deformation, contact time, and energy retention
weaknesses: Background citation breadth is limited and repeats ideas; lacks broader literature on ball elasticity
Omissions in materials list (e.g., pressure gauge accuracy, tape type) hinder exact replication
Procedure numbering is inconsistent and some steps (camera distance, frame rate, drop-height consistency) remain ambiguous
Rationale for neglecting drag force is not justified with quantitative estimates
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work meets the upper end of the 3â4 band: the research question is in a well-defined context (band 5â6 descriptor), and the methodology allows reproduction with few ambiguities (band 3â4). Methodological considerations are described but not fully explained. Minor stylistic issues and missing technical details preclude a top-band mark. A score of 4 fairly balances these strengths and omissions.

strengths: Conclusion correctly interprets the relationship between internal pressure and rebound height, linking back to the hypothesis
Quantitative comparisons (e.g., bounce height decrements) are used to support qualitative explanations
Discussion invokes deformation and energy dissipation consistent with the processed results
weaknesses: Conclusion is cut off mid-sentence and ends abruptly, indicating incompleteness
Comparison to theoretical or published COR values is purely qualitative; lacks engagement with external literature
Claims of trend linearity are made without testing alternative models or fitting data trends
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion answers the research question and is broadly consistent with the analysis (band 3â4), but its abrupt ending, lack of deeper scientific context, and overgeneralisation of trends limit its depth. The work aligns with the descriptor âdescribed but not fully consistent,â justifying a score of 3.

strengths: Evaluation identifies specific methodological weaknesses (surface roughness, unintended spin, manual video analysis)
Proposes realistic improvements directly tied to identified limitations (smoother ball surface, mechanical release, motion-tracking software)
weaknesses: Does not quantify the relative impact of each limitation on results
Improvement suggestions lack explanation of how much uncertainty or bias each change will reduce
Details on software choice, calibration procedures, and expected error reduction are omitted
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation describes specific methodological limitations and relevant improvements (band 3â4) but stops short of explaining their relative impacts or providing quantitative rationale. It meets the descriptor ârelevant improvements are described,â warranting a mid-band score of 3.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is narrowly focused on one quantitative variable (internal pressure) and two measurable outcomes (rebound height and coefficient of restitution) for an ordinary size-5 football.  The introduction links the problem to real-life training situations, briefly cites typical pressure ranges for other balls (basketball) and outlines the mechanical concepts involved (inelastic collisions, energy transformations).  These details give the reader a clear, specific, and authentic context for why the investigation matters and how it sits within mechanics; therefore the descriptor for 5â6 is met.  Minor stylistic repetition and limited citation breadth prevent a convincing award of the top mark of 6.

The student states instrumental uncertainties, shows one worked example of absolute and fractional uncertainty, and quotes uncertainties for averages and COR in the tables.  However, several calculations are incorrect (e.g., fractional uncertainty for COR set to 1, absolute COR uncertainty unrealistically equal to the value itself) and error bars are omitted from graphs despite being small but non-zero.  Consideration is therefore present but contains significant omissions and inaccuracies, matching the middle of band 3-4.

The student identifies relevant considerationsâtrial repetition, six well-spaced pressure points, multiple bounces, control of drop height, surface, ball size and spinâand justifies each in a control-variables table.  However, the rationale stops at description; there is little explanation of how each factor quantitatively influences data sufficiency or quality (e.g., why three trials are enough, or how bounce count affects precision).  Thus the work satisfies the descriptor âdescribedâ but not the deeper âexplained,â positioning it in the middle of the 3-4 band.

The evaluation table identifies concrete weaknessesâsurface damage on the ball, unintended spin, manual video analysisâand briefly states why each can affect results.  Yet the student stops short of analysing the magnitude or relative impact of these limitations on the final conclusion.  Hence the strand meets the descriptor "describes specific methodological weaknesses" without fully explaining their impact, meriting the lower end of band 3-4.

The student supplies a numbered procedure, a full materials list, and a table of controlled variables.  Key details such as pressure values, number of trials, bounce count, camera use, and ruler alignment are included, making replication largely possible.  Ambiguities remain (e.g., exact cameraâball distance, frame rate, how the drop height is guaranteed to be 1.00 m every time, how videos are analysed frame-by-frame), but these would only create minor clarifications for another experimenter.  Hence the work fits âallows the investigation to be reproduced with few ambiguities,â meriting the upper end of the 3-4 band.

Data processing includes averaging, graphing trends and computing coefficients of restitution from rebound heights.  The mathematical relationship used (vââh leading to e = â(h_f/h_i)) is appropriate.  Nonetheless, rounding inconsistencies, copy-paste transcription errors, and the incorrect propagation of uncertainty reduce the overall accuracy.  Processing is sound in principle but marred by noticeable inaccuracies, rightly earning the lower half of the 3-4 band.

Proposed improvements (use a smoother ball, two-hand or mechanical release, motion-tracking software) are realistic and clearly linked to the cited weaknesses.  Nevertheless, explanations of HOW each change would quantitatively enhance reliability or validity are minimal.  The work therefore satisfies ârelevant improvements are describedâ but does not reach the explanatory depth of the 5-6 band.

Raw-data tables include units, significant figures and stated instrumental uncertainty (Â±0.1 kPa, Â±0.50 cm).  Processed tables list averages and derived COR values.  Graphs are titled and axes labelled, colours distinguish pressure levels.  While table formatting is cluttered and some headings misaligned, the reader can still clearly follow how data were recorded and processed.  Both clarity and reasonable numerical precision are therefore achieved, warranting the upper end of the 3-4 band.

The student compares findings to theoretical expectations of energy conservation, Newtonâs third law and known limits of the coefficient of restitution (0â¤eâ¤1).  However, the comparison is qualitative and lacks engagement with external literature values for footballs or published studies.  The discussion is relevant but not deeply developed, corresponding to the middle of the 3-4 band.

The conclusion directly answers the research question, cites specific numerical trends from Graph 3 and Table 1, and links them to the hypothesis.  Explanations invoke deformation, contact time and energy dissipation, aligning logically with the processed results.  While minor over-generalisation (claiming linearity where plots appear slightly curvilinear) exists, the conclusion remains broadly consistent and well-justified by the analysis, fitting the upper part of the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: The hypothesis articulates causal mechanisms linking pressure, deformation, contact time, and energy retention, demonstrating strong conceptual understanding.
The materials list omits specifications such as pressure gauge accuracy and tape type; include these details for precise replication.
Step numbering restarts at 1 under Procedure, conflicting with earlier counts. Ensure consistent step labels to avoid confusion.
Procedure references repeating steps 5 and 6 for trials, but numbering is inconsistent. Revisit sequence numbering and clarify the trial protocol.
Raw data tables consistently include uncertainties, units, and significant figures, enabling clear and precise communication of measurements.
Column headings in the raw data table are crowded under merged cells; splitting headings or adjusting cell borders would improve readability.
Qualitative observations note horizontal motion but do not quantify its frequency or impact; consider logging the number of misaligned trials for statistical context.
Calculated fractional uncertainty for the coefficient of restitution is set to 1, indicating misapplication of propagation rules. Recalculate using correct formulas.
Averaged rebound heights with uncertainties are tabulated clearly, providing a solid foundation for trend analysis.
Worked examples for average height and COR are detailed step-by-step, which helps transparency of data processing.
Absolute uncertainty calculation uses only two trials; ensure to compute maxâmin over all trials and apply fractional uncertainty consistently.
Fractional uncertainty for e is computed incorrectly, resulting in a nonsensical value of 1. Revisit the propagation formula and input correct values.
The research question is clearly stated, focusing on internal pressure effects on rebound height and coefficient of restitution and grounded in a concrete, sports-related context.
The introduction repeats ideas and lacks broader literature citations; consider integrating recent studies on ball elasticity to deepen context and demonstrate research breadth.
The background states drag force is negligible without justification; include a brief estimate or condition under which air resistance can be ignored to support this assumption.
Including basketball dribbling examples with specific pressure ranges enriches context and shows interdisciplinary linkage between mechanics and realâworld sports applications.
Equating normal force to weight during impact overlooks elastic compression; clarify force distribution during contact rather than simplifying normal and weight forces.
The derivation of $$e=\sqrt{\frac{h_{final}}{h_{initial}}}$$ from energy conservation is mathematically sound and clearly presented.
The rationale for choosing six pressure values could be strengthened by explaining how this range and spacing optimize trend resolution or statistical significance.
Specify camera settings such as frame rate, resolution, and distance from the ruler to justify measurement precision and improve reproducibility.
The controlâvariable table is comprehensive, but merged cell headings are misaligned, making it harder to scan. Adjust column formatting for better readability.
Describing dropping while kneeling and extending the arm lacks precision. A fixed mechanical release mechanism would ensure consistent drop height.
Stating uncertainties are too small to show on graphs undermines the importance of error bars; even small uncertainties should be displayed or discussed.
Graph 1 lacks axis labels with units and numerical scales are not annotated. Add axis ticks, units, and a clear legend to enhance interpretability.
The text describes an inverse relationship but does not quantify correlation strength; consider fitting a trendline and reporting $$R^2$$ to support claims.
The student correctly interprets Graph 3, identifying a direct relationship between internal pressure and rebound height, aligning conclusion with data.
Comparison to theoretical models is qualitative; integrating literature values for standard football COR would strengthen scientific context and validate findings.
Claiming a logarithmic trend for CoR vs pressure requires testing alternative models; fit multiple functions and compare fits to justify the chosen model.
The conclusion is cut off mid-sentence and ends abruptly. Ensure full articulation of concluding remarks and check for completeness.
The student integrates quantitative comparisons of bounce height decrements to explain exponential decay, linking data and theory effectively.
Evaluation acknowledges surface roughness and spin issues but lacks discussion of their relative impacts; identify which limitation most affects results and quantify why.
Improvement suggestions are realistic but omit how much uncertainty each change would reduce; provide estimates or rationale for expected reliability gains.
The proposal to use tracking software is sound but lacks detail on software choice, calibration procedures, and expected error reduction. Specify tools and methods.
Bibliography lists a single source; extend references to include primary literature on COR measurements in sports equipment for stronger academic grounding.
The initial schematic diagram lacks a caption and axis labels, hindering interpretation. Add descriptive captions and units to all figures for clarity.

rubricData.name: Physics IA
rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
